title: Net official development assistance to medical research and basic health sectors
subtitle: Net [official development assistance](#dod:oda) to medical research and basic health sectors. This data is expressed in US dollars. It is adjusted for inflation but does not account for differences in living costs between countries.
caption:
Net official development assistance to medical research and basic health sectors, 2023

Net official development assistance to medical research and basic health sectors. This data is expressed in US dollars. It is adjusted for inflation but does not account for differences in living costs between countries.

The choropleth map displays the distribution of net official development assistance directed toward medical research and basic health sectors across the globe for the year 2023, with values expressed in constant 2022 US dollars. The visualization utilizes a continuous color scale ranging from pale yellow, representing zero dollars, to deep navy blue, indicating amounts exceeding 300 million dollars. Countries shaded in grey, such as the United States, Canada, Australia, and most of Western Europe, represent regions where no data is reported for this specific metric, likely because they are donors rather than recipients. The highest concentrations of assistance appear as dark blue patches across sub-Saharan Africa and parts of South Asia. Specifically, Ethiopia, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Tanzania, and Nigeria fall into the highest visible band, receiving over 300 million dollars each. India and Pakistan also display dark blue shading, placing them in this top tier of funding receipt. Moving down the scale, countries like Kenya, Uganda, and Mozambique appear in medium-to-dark blue tones, suggesting receipt levels between 100 million and 300 million dollars. A broad band of nations in South America, including Brazil and Peru, along with countries in North Africa and Southeast Asia like Indonesia and the Philippines, are colored in lighter teal and green shades, corresponding to the 10 million to 100 million dollar range. The lowest non-zero values, represented by pale yellow and light green, are visible in nations such as Mexico, Chile, and various states in Central Asia and the Middle East. The geographic pattern shows a heavy concentration of financial flows toward the African continent and the Indian subcontinent, while the Americas and Oceania show significantly lower or unreported figures for this specific category of health sector support.
